model_narrative: We plan electricity generation across a set of generation units, each able to serve certain segments of the demand profile. For each unit and the load areas it is allowed to serve, we decide how much electricity it produces over the year. Producing electricity carries a per-unit cost, generates CO2 emissions, and some units count as domestically sourced endogenous generation. We track total cost, total emissions, and total endogenous production as accounting quantities. The objective is to make the total production cost as small as possible.

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the power generation units, each a technology that can produce electricity

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the load areas, the segments of the demand profile to be served

sets.name: pi
sets.doc: the feasible unit-load pairs; a pair is present only when that generation unit is allowed to serve that load area, so production is defined only over these pairs

sets.name: es
sets.doc: the subset of generation units that count as endogenous sources, that is domestically sourced generation

params.name: ad
params.doc: the total annual electricity demand to be met, in GWh

params.name: df
params.doc: the fraction of total annual demand that falls in each load area, a dimensionless share that sums to one over the load areas

params.name: capacity
params.doc: the maximum annual production each generation unit can deliver across all the load areas it serves, in GWh

params.name: cost
params.doc: the production cost of each generation unit, in dollars per MWh

params.name: co2
params.doc: the CO2 emission factor of each generation unit, in tons per MWh

params.name: eps_co2
params.doc: the upper bound allowed on total CO2 emissions, an eps-constraint bound carried over from the multiobjective sweep

params.name: eps_es
params.doc: the lower bound required on total endogenous production, an eps-constraint bound carried over from the multiobjective sweep

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the production level of each feasible unit-load pair, in GWh

vars.name: z_cost
vars.doc: the total production cost objective value

vars.name: z_co2
vars.doc: the total CO2 emissions objective value

vars.name: z_es
vars.doc: the total endogenous production objective value

constraints.name: cost[p]
constraints.intent: Total production cost adds up the cost of every unit's production across the load areas it serves. For each feasible unit-load pair, value its production at that unit's per-unit cost, and set the total cost accounting quantity equal to the sum of these amounts over all the pairs.

constraints.name: co2[p]
constraints.intent: Total CO2 emissions add up the emissions from every unit's production across the load areas it serves. For each feasible unit-load pair, multiply its production by that unit's emission factor, and set the total emissions accounting quantity equal to the sum of these amounts over all the pairs.

constraints.name: x[p,
constraints.intent: Total endogenous production adds up the production of only the units that count as endogenous sources, across the load areas they serve. Set the total endogenous accounting quantity equal to the sum of production over the feasible unit-load pairs whose unit is an endogenous source.

constraints.name: x[pp,
constraints.intent: Each generation unit has a limited annual output. For each unit, the total production it delivers summed over the load areas it serves must not exceed that unit's capacity.

constraints.name: x[p,
constraints.intent: Demand in each load area must be met. For each load area, the total production delivered to it summed over the units that serve it must be at least the share of total annual demand that falls in that load area.

constraints.name: eps_con_co2
constraints.intent: Total CO2 emissions must stay within the allowed emissions ceiling. The total emissions accounting quantity must not exceed the CO2 upper bound.

constraints.name: eps_con_es
constraints.intent: Total endogenous production must meet the required endogenous floor. The total endogenous accounting quantity must be at least the endogenous lower bound.

query: We're looking at a renewables mandate — at least 5000 GWh has to come from the RES units across the year. What does the optimizer return for total cost once that floor is in?
expected_pyomo: model.res_floor = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['RES', i] for (p, i) in model.pi if p == 'RES') >= 5000.0)

query: Suppose oil availability gets squeezed and we can only count on the oil units for 8000 GWh total. What's the cost picture under that limit?
expected_pyomo: model.oil_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['Oil', i] for (p, i) in model.pi if p == 'Oil') <= 8000.0)

query: There's talk of throttling the gas fleet for grid-stability reasons — hold total gas generation to no more than 18000 GWh. Where does the total cost land if we do that?
expected_pyomo: model.gas_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['Gas', i] for (p, i) in model.pi if p == 'Gas') <= 18000.0)

query: The plan leans hard on lignite even though it's our dirtiest unit. Shouldn't we hold total lignite generation under 25000 GWh? What's the model seeing that makes it push lignite that hard?
expected_pyomo: model.lig_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['Lignite', i] for (p, i) in model.pi if p == 'Lignite') <= 25000.0)

query: We've got 10000 GWh of clean RES capacity sitting essentially idle. Couldn't we just commit it to deliver at least 6000 GWh? What's keeping the optimizer from using the renewables?
expected_pyomo: model.res_use = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['RES', i] for (p, i) in model.pi if p == 'RES') >= 6000.0)

query: The oil units barely show up in the schedule. Couldn't we just put them to work and run at least 13000 GWh of oil? What's pulling the plan away from oil?
expected_pyomo: model.oil_floor = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['Oil', i] for (p, i) in model.pi if p == 'Oil') >= 13000.0)
